title: geum aleppicum subvar. hirsutum
intro: Geum aleppicum subvar. hirsutum, also known as woolly avens, is a perennial plant species in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of woolly avens include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Woolly avens can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
